$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; usesContext extended with queryId + artisanpack/postPreview so the block previews inside a query loop (#483). Attribute schema, supports, and selectors carried verbatim for parity. commentId is retained in usesContext for forward-compat with the comments family fork (#519).

Previews via the lightweight createEntityPlaceholderEdit helper (kind='image'). Renders the stamped _resolvedAuthorAvatar URL with _resolvedAuthorName alt text when present, otherwise a labelled placeholder. Upstream edit.js is intentionally NOT delegated: it queries the user entity through @wordpress/core-data, which the post editor does not populate. Front-end markup is produced server-side from the stamped _resolved* attributes.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from stamped _resolved* attributes.
Bidirectional core/avatar ↔ artisanpack/avatar block transforms for the rollout window. Upstream has no transforms.js for this block.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit rather than upstream edit.js — the core-data shim does not expose the user entity to the post editor. Live in-editor entity values would require expanding the shim (out of scope for the fork).
avatar is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolvedAuthorAvatar / _resolvedAuthorName attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/avatar during the V1 rollout.

Per-comment avatar resolution (commentId context, comment author lookup) is not yet implemented; the fork retains commentId in usesContext so the comments family fork (#519) can wire it up without a block.json bump. For now an avatar block placed inside a post-context loop renders the post author's avatar; outside a post context it renders nothing.
